headline: Off-Duty Firefighter Makes 'Grab of His Career' Rescuing 3 Children from Overturned Boat
meta_description: Jason Horne, off duty on July 4th, dove under a capsized pontoon boat on Alvarado Lake to rescue three trapped children, performing CPR until first responders…
summary_short: An off-duty North Texas firefighter rescued three children trapped beneath an overturned pontoon boat on Independence Day, performing life-saving CPR on the water.
body:
On the Fourth of July, Jason Horne was spending time with his 12-year-old daughter on Alvarado Lake in Midlothian when he spotted a capsized pontoon boat in the distance. What began as a family outing became a race against time: three children were trapped underneath. Without hesitation, Horne told his daughter to stay put, jumped into the water, and swam toward the overturned vessel. "The people were all yelling, 'Our babies are under there,'" he recalled. "So, I went around to the side of the boat, dove underneath."

Two children emerged from beneath the pontoon—a boy who was dizzy but breathing, and a girl who was not. Horne pulled the girl onto the boat and began chest compressions. Learning that a third child remained trapped below, he dove back into the water. With help from Good Samaritans who arrived at the scene, a second boy was found entangled in ropes and pulled to safety. Horne continued performing CPR on the boy during the journey back to the boat ramp, where first responders were waiting. All three children recovered after being rushed to the hospital, according to a news release from the Alvarado Fire Chief.

What makes Horne's actions remarkable isn't just the lives saved—it's his humility about it all. The family reached out personally to thank their rescuer, but Horne insisted he was simply doing what anyone would do. "I was just trying to help those people," he said. In firefighting terminology, pulling someone from danger is called "making a grab." By his own account, Horne made the grab of his career—and he wasn't even wearing the uniform.
quick_facts: Jason Horne rescued 3 children from overturned pontoon boat on July 4th
Incident occurred on Alvarado Lake in Midlothian, North Texas
Horne performed CPR while heading back to boat ramp
All three children recovered after hospitalization
Horne was off duty and with his 12-year-old daughter
